items.summary: Cracking the whip on non-scheduled carriers, aviation regulator DGCA has rolled out a fresh set of stringent safety norms amid rising concerns over operational discipline in the charter and private aviation segment.

items.title: Banks' liquidity buffers shrink as deposits lag credit growth
items.summary: Banks are confronting mounting liquidity pressures as loan portfolios expand faster than deposit bases. As a result, liquidity coverage ratios have weakened across a broad set of institutions. Even with abundant liquidity in the wider financial system, banks are struggling to bring in new deposits, pushing up near-term funding costs.

items.title: Chalet Hotels to open its first Ritz Carlton in Hyderabad
items.summary: Chalet Hotels is set to launch India's first Ritz Carlton in Hyderabad, a 330-room luxury hotel project costing approximately ₹630 crore. This venture, a collaboration with Marriott International, marks Chalet's entry into ultra-luxury hospitality. The company anticipates strong rental performance and good returns from this strategic expansion in a thriving market.

items.title: Apple shifts Mac mini production to US, deepens $600 billion investment amid Trump's manufacturing push
items.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook said the Mac mini will be produced in the US later this year as part of a $600 billion investment plan. The company is expanding operations in Houston, boosting chip sourcing from US partners, and opening a new manufacturing center to strengthen domestic production

items.title: India’s real estate sector embraces artificial intelligence to woo tech-savvy buyers
items.summary: India's real estate sector is rapidly embracing AI to enhance luxury living and operational efficiency. From biometric access and predictive maintenance in homes to streamlining office design and improving construction cycles, AI is transforming how properties are discovered, evaluated, and managed, catering to a new generation of tech-savvy wealth creators.

items.title: Govt tweaks Build Operate Transfer agreement to share risks and rewards with contractors'
items.summary: New Delhi: The Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways, Nitin Gadkari, announced modifications to the Build Operate Transfer road project agreement. This change aims for equitable risk and reward sharing between the government and concessionaires. Over ₹2 lakh crore in BOT road projects are planned.

items.title: Cabinet panel allows increased equity investment limit in Power Grid arms
items.summary: The government has boosted Power Grid Corporation's investment limit to ₹7,500 crore per subsidiary. This supports large projects and renewable energy. Additionally, three railway projects worth ₹9,072 crore are approved. These will decongest tracks and speed up transport. The projects will add 307 km to the network and handle more freight.

items.title: Broadcasters flag surge in DTH signal piracy, seek mandatory tech safeguards to curb losses
items.summary: India's broadcast industry is grappling with a significant surge in television signal piracy, with smuggled DTH set-top boxes illegally redistributing pay TV feeds across borders. This unauthorized redistribution is eroding revenues for legitimate broadcasters, prompting calls for mandatory technological safeguards like forensic watermarking to combat the escalating issue.

items.title: AI’s positives outweigh human and climate impact: NASSCOM
items.summary: On worries regarding the climate impact of data centres, NASSCOM said that the improvement in human conditions as an outcome of AI outweighs the adversarial climate impact.

items.title: IDFC First Bank Fraud: Haryana forms high-level panel to review state bank policy
items.summary: The Haryana government has set up a committee to look into unuthorised fund transfers in the IDFC First Bank fraud and to review the state bank policy.

items.summary: Larsen & Toubro (L&T) has secured a major order from India’s Department of Atomic Energy to construct a Laser Interferometer Gravitational Wave Observatory (LIGO) in Aundha, Hingoli, Maharashtra. The project, valued between ₹1,000-2,500 crore, includes high-precision civil infrastructure, an 8 km ultra-high vacuum beam tube, and critical vacuum equipment. Scheduled for completion in 48 months, this initiative is set to be one of India’s flagship ‘Mega Science’ projects.

items.title: Coal exchange rollout should be in phases: Coal India Limited
items.summary: Coal India envisions a phased National Coal Exchange. This new platform will shift from auctions to electronic trading. Market reforms will be carefully balanced with national energy security. Long-term fuel supply agreements will ensure stability for power producers. The exchange aims for transparent price discovery. Independent regulatory oversight is crucial for its success.

Young India shifts to solitaires as prices decline 30%
items.summary: Solitaire diamond rings are seeing a surge in demand among Indians aged 28-40, with sales rising 25-35% year-on-year following a 30% price drop. A 1-carat ring now costs ₹5-5.5 lakh, down from ₹7-8 lakh, making diamond-heavy, low-gold jewellery more appealing amid high gold prices. Millennials are driving growth, with engagements as the primary purchase but self-buys and milestone-driven purchases also rising. Lower entry barriers and a preference for accessible, meaningful jewellery, including 9-carat and 14-carat gold, are reshaping consumer behaviour, benefiting jewellers despite elevated g

items.title: ICAI’s new global networking rules unsettle Big 4, tighten compliance for audit affiliates
items.summary: India's new global networking rules for audit firms, aimed at boosting local players, have introduced stringent compliance and disclosure requirements. While allowing firms to join global networks, the norms mandate registration, appointment of a nodal officer, and arm's length dealings, causing disquiet among Big 4 and consultancy houses.

items.title: Wondrlab in talks to acquire Madison World from Sam Balsara in Rs 1,000 crore deal
items.summary: Marketing network Wondrlab is reportedly in the final stages of acquiring homegrown advertising major Madison World for approximately ₹1,000 crore. This significant deal, if completed, would be the largest acquisition of an independent Indian ad agency by a domestic firm, marking founder Sam Balsara's exit after nearly four decades.

items.title: Venezuela readies larger oil cargoes for exports, targets India
items.summary: Venezuelan oil exports are set to accelerate. The first Very Large Crude Carriers are now chartered for shipments. This development follows a recent deal between Venezuela and the United States. Deliveries to India are expected to increase significantly. Trading firms Vitol and Trafigura are involved in these operations. This marks a new phase for Venezuelan crude exports.

items.title: ESR enters data centre market with Rs 900 crore investment in Navi Mumbai
items.summary: ESR is making a major foray into India’s data centre space with a high-value investment in Navi Mumbai. The company will develop a large, multi-level campus in Rabale designed to handle intensive cloud and AI workloads. This initiative underscores India’s rising prominence in the global digital infrastructure landscape and is expected to stimulate local employment, technology adoption, and economic growth.

items.title: European Stocks Decline With Focus on New US Tariffs, Earnings
items.summary: European stocks fell as President Donald Trump’s new 10% global tariffs came into effect, adding to investor uncertainty amid mixed earnings reports.

items.title: European Stocks Steady as Banks Retreat and Utilities Gain
items.summary: European stock indexes were muted, with bank stocks sliding after JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s Jamie Dimon said he’s seeing parallels to the era before the 2008 financial crisis, while utilities and chemical makers advanced on positive earnings.

items.summary: Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan announced approval of gram, mustard and lentil procurement under the Price Support Scheme for Rabi 2026, reviewed Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana progress, and urged states to fully utilise central funds before March 31 to safeguard farmer interests.

items.title: Merck & Co to create a separate cancer unit as patent cliff looms
items.summary: Merck & Co is restructuring by splitting its drug division into two separate businesses. The change is designed to spotlight its fastest-growing medicines as the blockbuster cancer therapy Keytruda nears patent expiration. The company plans to emphasize newer products, including a pneumonia vaccine and a treatment for lung disease, as key drivers of its next growth phase.

items.summary: The government has clarified that reduced rates and value caps under the RoDTEP scheme do not apply to agricultural and processed food exports. This clarification follows a recent decision to halve duty benefits for exporters. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade issued a notification stating Chapters 1 to 24 of the ITC HS code are exempt.

items.title: MoSPI holds workshop on leveraging administrative data for governance
items.summary: Government departments are uniting to build interconnected, AI-ready data systems for better governance. A national workshop emphasized harmonizing state-level data, breaking silos, and expanding access. This initiative aims to strengthen evidence-based policymaking and democratize intelligence for real-time decision-making at all levels, paving the way for the AI revolution.

items.title: Government blocks 5 OTT platforms for streaming obscene content
items.summary: New Delhi authorities have taken decisive action against five OTT platforms. MoodXVIP, Koyal Playpro, Digi Movieplex, Feel, and Jugnu have been blocked. This move follows established procedures under the Information Technology Rules 2021. The government aims to uphold public decency and national interests. This action is in line with the Information Technology Act 2000.

items.summary: Shares of US software firms partnered with AI startup Anthropic surged, leading a sector rebound, following fears over AI's disruptive impact. Details are here.

items.title: Jharkhand plane crash: RedBird Airways says there were no defects with air ambulance
items.summary: RedBird Airways has stated there were no defects with the aircraft that crashed in Jharkhand. The company also confirmed the pilots were experienced. The air ambulance was en route from Ranchi to Delhi when it crashed, killing all seven people on board. The probe agency AAIB has seized documents from RedBird Airways' office. The investigation into the incident is ongoing.

items.title: WTO establishes penal on India-China dispute on PLI
items.summary: The World Trade Organisation has agreed to form a dispute panel. China filed a case against India concerning its automotive and renewable energy policies. Beijing claims India's incentive schemes discriminate against foreign businesses. India maintains its measures comply with WTO rules. This development follows earlier consultations between the two nations. The US also commented on China's decision.

items.title: DGCA set to overhaul charter flight safety after 2 fatal crashes
items.summary: Aviation watchdog DGCA is implementing stringent safety measures for non-scheduled flight operators. This follows recent plane crashes. Measures include public disclosure of aircraft maintenance history and a safety ranking system. Pilots' decisions to divert or cancel flights for safety will be respected.

items.title: Business Jet Operators' Association asks business jet operators to comply with safety culture
items.summary: Following two fatal accidents involving non-scheduled flight operators, the Business Jet Operators' Association (BAOA) urged its members to conduct internal safety audits and reinforce crew briefings. This call comes after a Redbird Airways aircraft crashed in Jharkhand, killing all onboard, and a previous Learjet incident in January.

items.title: Trai paves way for over  ₹2 trillion spectrum auction, cuts reserve price
items.summary: The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (Trai) has recommended up to a 20-30% reduction in reserve price for the spectrum compared to the previous auction in 2024.

items.title: DGCA takes action against VSR Ventures for various lapses; grounds 4 planes
items.summary: DGCA has grounded four Learjet 40/45 aircraft operated by VSR Ventures after a safety audit found multiple compliance lapses in maintenance and flight operations. The action follows last month’s Baramati crash that killed Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Ajit Pawar and four others.

items.title: Jammu–Srinagar in 5 Hours: Vande Bharat Express extended to Jammu Tawi from March 1; check key details
items.summary: Responding to the demand of the people, Railways has now extended Vande Bharat trains up to Jammu, ensuring seamless connectivity between Jammu and the Kashmir Valley. This milestone reflects the continued commitment of the Government of India and Indian Railways towards enhancing infrastructure and fulfilling the aspirations of the people of Jammu & Kashmir.

items.title: Indian pharma leaders call for collaboration, partnerships to spur innovation
items.summary: The discussion, featuring Shanghvi alongside Aurobindo Pharma whole-time director Madan Mohan Reddy, Lupin managing director Nilesh Gupta and Zydus Lifesciences managing director Sharvil Patel, focused on a growing consensus: Indian pharma must move up the value chain to develop innovative drugs.

items.title: Jharkhand air ambulance crash: DGCA teams start probe, bodies handed over to families
items.summary: Two DGCA teams began probing the crash of a Redbird Airways Beechcraft C90 air ambulance in Jharkhand’s Chatra district that killed seven, including two pilots and a patient. The aircraft lost contact after take-off from Ranchi, with inclement weather suspected as a possible cause.

items.title: US software stocks climb as Anthropic announcement sparks relief rally
items.summary: Software stocks saw a strong rebound on Tuesday. This followed news of new AI tools developed by Anthropic with its partners. These tools aim to assist in areas like investment banking and HR. Shares of companies like LSEG, FactSet, Salesforce's Slack, and DocuSign saw gains. This positive movement comes after recent fears about AI's impact on the sector.

The investment targets a Grade A, fully occupied asset with strong rental yields, aligning with Nisus Finance's broader UAE real estate deployment strategy.

items.title: WTO sets up panel in China's case against India's certain auto, renewable measures
items.summary: The WTO's dispute settlement body has established a panel to examine China's complaint against India's automotive and renewable energy sector policies. Beijing alleges that India's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes discriminate against Chinese goods, violating global trade rules. Bilateral consultations failed to resolve the dispute, prompting China's second request for a panel.

items.summary: Dhoot Transmission has partnered with FourFront, a leading automotive electronics supplier, in a strategic move to bolster its electrical and electronics capabilities. items.summary: Under this agreement, the company will establish two major projects at Noida International Airport in Jewar, located in Gautam Buddha Nagar. One project will be a state-of-the-art cargo campus, and the other will be a world-class air catering kitchen. AISATS will invest Rs 4458 crore in these two projects.

items.title: Indian economy now more resilient to shocks, says RBI deputy governor Poonam Gupta
items.summary: RBI Deputy Governor Poonam Gupta stated India's economy is now more resilient to internal and external shocks, fostering macroeconomic stability and an improving growth trajectory. She highlighted a "virtuous cycle of accelerated growth and macroeconomic stability," supported by a robust banking sector and expanding non-banking space.

items.title: Voltas expects summer demand to revert to 2024 levels
items.summary: Voltas anticipates a strong summer season ahead. Demand is expected to rebound to 2024 levels after a dip last year. The company projects a 15-20% rise in domestic sales.
